rookie.title: Safari Tragedy
rookie.flavor: The morning game drive turned deadly when international soccer star Kylie Simpson was found trampled near the watering hole. What seemed like a wildlife accident has darker undertones in the savanna heat.
rookie.emptyState: The Big Five became the Final One.

items.name: Marcus Webb
items.detail: Wildlife photographer capturing the safari experience

items.name: Dr. Elena Vasquez
items.detail: Veterinarian monitoring animal health on the reserve

items.name: Thomas Mwangi
items.detail: Safari guide with twenty years local experience

items.name: Watering Hole
items.detail: Primary gathering spot for wildlife during dry season

items.name: Acacia Grove
items.detail: Dense tree cluster providing shade and cover

items.name: Safari Camp
items.detail: Tourist accommodation with luxury tented suites

items.name: Elephant Stampede
items.detail: Massive herd spooked into defensive charging behavior

items.name: Rhino Gore
items.detail: Aggressive territorial male defending watering rights

items.name: Vehicle Collision
items.detail: Safari jeep impact during emergency wildlife encounter

clues.heading: Footprint Pattern
clues.text: Deep boot impressions lead from camp toward the watering hole, matching Thomas Mwangi's distinctive hiking boots.
clues.alone: Thomas walked to the scene before the incident.

links.insight: Thomas was at the watering hole when the elephants charged, making him responsible for the stampede that killed Kylie.

clues.heading: Guest Testimony
clues.text: Marcus Webb was seen arguing loudly with Kylie about exclusive photo rights just hours before her death.
clues.alone: Marcus had a recent conflict with the victim.

links.insight: Despite their argument, Marcus was photographing birds miles away when Kylie died, eliminating him as the killer.

clues.heading: Medical Equipment
clues.text: Dr. Vasquez's tranquilizer gun shows recent use, with one dart missing from her standard field kit.
clues.alone: Elena used her tranquilizer recently for unknown purposes.

links.insight: The missing tranquilizer dart was used to sedate an injured zebra, not to harm Kylie, clearing Elena of suspicion.

clues.heading: Elephant Dung
clues.text: Fresh elephant droppings contain traces of Thomas's energy bar wrapper, indicating the herd was agitated by human presence.
clues.alone: Thomas accidentally provoked the elephant herd.

links.insight: Thomas was at the watering hole when the elephants charged, making him responsible for the stampede that killed Kylie.

clues.heading: Camera Timestamps
clues.text: Marcus's camera shows continuous bird photography taken fifteen miles north during Kylie's estimated time of death.
clues.alone: Marcus was far from the scene when Kylie died.

links.insight: Despite their argument, Marcus was photographing birds miles away when Kylie died, eliminating him as the killer.

clues.heading: Injured Zebra
clues.text: Camp staff confirm Dr. Vasquez was treating a zebra with a broken leg using tranquilizers throughout the morning.
clues.alone: Elena's tranquilizer use was for legitimate veterinary work.

links.insight: The missing tranquilizer dart was used to sedate an injured zebra, not to harm Kylie, clearing Elena of suspicion.

rookie.epilogue: Thomas Mwangi never intended to harm anyone when he dropped his energy bar wrapper near the elephants. The veteran guide's moment of carelessness at the watering hole triggered a deadly stampede. His twenty years of experience couldn't undo one tragic mistake that cost Kylie Simpson her life. Now Thomas faces the devastating reality that his negligence turned the African savanna into a crime scene.

clues.heading: Footprint Pattern
clues.text: Deep boot impressions lead from camp toward the watering hole, matching Thomas Mwangi's distinctive hiking boots.
clues.alone: Thomas walked to the scene before the incident.

links.insight: Thomas was at the watering hole during morning hours when the elephants charged, making him responsible for the stampede that killed Kylie.

clues.heading: Misleading Account
clues.text: Camp manager insists Sarah Chen was filming at the observation deck all morning, providing her a solid alibi.
clues.alone: Sarah appears to have been safely filming elsewhere.

links.insight: The camp manager was mistaken about Sarah's location - she was actually at the watering hole during the critical morning window when Kylie died.

clues.heading: Medical Equipment
clues.text: Dr. Vasquez's tranquilizer gun shows recent use, with one dart missing from her standard field kit.
clues.alone: Elena used her tranquilizer recently for unknown purposes.

links.insight: Elena's tranquilizer use occurred during midday heat, hours after the dawn incident, eliminating her from the critical morning window.

clues.heading: Elephant Dung
clues.text: Fresh elephant droppings contain traces of Thomas's energy bar wrapper, indicating the herd was agitated by human presence.
clues.alone: Thomas accidentally provoked the elephant herd.

links.insight: Thomas was at the watering hole during morning hours when the elephants charged, making him responsible for the stampede that killed Kylie.

clues.heading: Camera Memory Card
clues.text: Sarah's filming equipment was found at the watering hole with footage timestamp showing 7:30 AM recording.
clues.alone: Sarah was actually filming at the death scene.

links.insight: The camp manager was mistaken about Sarah's location - she was actually at the watering hole during the critical morning window when Kylie died.

clues.heading: Veterinary Log
clues.text: Staff records show Dr. Vasquez treated an injured zebra at 12:15 PM, accounting for the missing tranquilizer.
clues.alone: Elena's tranquilizer use was documented medical work.

links.insight: Elena's tranquilizer use occurred during midday heat, hours after the dawn incident, eliminating her from the critical morning window.

detective.epilogue: Thomas Mwangi never intended to harm anyone when he dropped his energy bar wrapper near the elephants during the busy morning game drive. The veteran guide's moment of carelessness at the watering hole triggered a deadly stampede during the peak wildlife viewing hours. His twenty years of experience couldn't undo one tragic mistake that cost Kylie Simpson her life. Now Thomas faces the devastating reality that his negligence during the morning hours turned the African savanna into a crime scene.

clues.heading: Footprint Pattern
clues.text: Deep boot impressions lead from camp toward the watering hole, matching Thomas Mwangi's distinctive hiking boots.
clues.alone: Thomas walked to the scene before the incident.

links.insight: Thomas was at the watering hole during morning hours when the elephants charged, making him responsible for the stampede that killed Kylie.

clues.heading: Official Statement
clues.text: James Okoye claims he spent the morning reviewing conservation permits at the ranger station, supporting park policy.
clues.alone: James appears focused on legitimate government work.

links.insight: Despite his claims of supporting conservation, James was secretly undermining Kylie's efforts due to conflicts with his development plans, but this doesn't make him the killer.

clues.heading: Medical Equipment
clues.text: Dr. Elena Vasquez's tranquilizer gun shows recent use, with one dart missing from her standard field kit.
clues.alone: Elena used her tranquilizer recently for unknown purposes.

links.insight: Elena's missing tranquilizer was used for legitimate veterinary work treating injured wildlife, driven by her dedication to animal care rather than any harmful intent.

clues.heading: Elephant Dung
clues.text: Fresh elephant droppings contain traces of Thomas's energy bar wrapper, indicating the herd was agitated by human presence.
clues.alone: Thomas accidentally provoked the elephant herd.

links.insight: Thomas was at the watering hole during morning hours when the elephants charged, making him responsible for the stampede that killed Kylie.

clues.heading: Permit Documents
clues.text: Hidden files reveal James opposed Kylie's conservation campaign, which threatened his undisclosed development contracts worth millions.
clues.alone: James had strong financial reasons to want Kylie silenced.

links.insight: Despite his claims of supporting conservation, James was secretly undermining Kylie's efforts due to conflicts with his development plans, but this doesn't make him the killer.

clues.heading: Veterinary Log
clues.text: Staff records show Dr. Vasquez treated an injured zebra at 12:15 PM, driven by her passionate commitment to wildlife welfare.
clues.alone: Elena's tranquilizer use reflected her dedication to animal care.

links.insight: Elena's missing tranquilizer was used for legitimate veterinary work treating injured wildlife, driven by her dedication to animal care rather than any harmful intent.

inspector.epilogue: Thomas Mwangi never intended to harm anyone when he carelessly dropped his energy bar wrapper near the elephants during the busy morning game drive at the watering hole. The veteran guide's moment of accidental negligence triggered a deadly stampede during the peak wildlife viewing hours. His twenty years of experience couldn't undo one tragic mistake that cost Kylie Simpson her life. Now Thomas faces the devastating reality that his unintended carelessness during the morning hours turned the African savanna into a crime scene.
